consistency.naming_convention: Consistent with standard D&D feature naming (Title Case).
consistency.structure: Standard feature JSON structure (slug, name, class, level, description, is_subclass_feature).

quality.clarity: The description is clear and concise.
quality.completeness: It fully describes the mechanical benefit: allowing a musical instrument to substitute for a spellcasting focus.

mechanics.effect: Grants the ability to use a musical instrument as a spellcasting focus for bard spells.
mechanics.rules_context: This feature directly relates to the core spellcasting rules (PHB/Basic Rules) regarding spell components.
mechanics.balance_assessment: Appropriate for a Level 1 feature. It simplifies spellcasting for Bards without adding direct power increase (it's a convenience/flavor feature, as Bard instruments often function as focuses anyway, but this codifies it explicitly for the class).
mechanics.potential_ambiguity: None, as the definition of a 'Spellcasting Focus' is well-established in the system rules.

recommendations.data_refinement: No necessary refinements. The inclusion of the markdown link to '[musical instrument](#section-tools)' is helpful for internal navigation/contextual reference.
recommendations.schema_validation: Passes standard schema validation for class features.

raw_data_validation.name: Spellcasting Focus
raw_data_validation.description: You can use a [musical instrument](#section-tools) as a spellcasting focus for your bard spells.
